mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly positive when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO trends upward in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0 percent forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold shows no out-of-sample predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and rising consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales signal synchronized strength in both durable goods and consumer discretionary sectors, driving broad market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative or null Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns fail to exceed baseline when both series trend upward simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising federal funds rates indicates a supply-side contraction exacerbated by monetary tightening, which suppresses corporate output and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or walk-forward testing shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.10) during stagflationary periods.
